lines.english: He administers justice, and is absorbed in the spiritual wisdom of the Guru.
The Primal Lord is everywhere, immaculate and all-knowing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਲੈ ਗਰਦਨਿ ਮਾਰੇ ਹਉਮੈ ਲੋਭੁ ਚੁਕਾਇਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh lai garadan maare haumai lobh chukaaeaa |6|
lines.english: He seizes sexual desire and anger by their necks, and kills them; He eradicates egotism and greed. ||6||
Whoever understands his own self, contemplates the Word of the Shabad.
In the True Place, the Formless Lord abides.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੈ ਮਹਲਿ ਨਿਵਾਸੁ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣੁ ਚੁਕਾਇਆ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: sachai mehal nivaas nirantar aavan jaan chukaaeaa |7|
lines.english: He comes to abide deep within the True Mansion of His Presence, and his comings and goings are ended. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਮਨੁ ਚਲੈ ਨ ਪਉਣੁ ਉਡਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: naa man chalai na paun uddaavai |
lines.english: His mind does not waver, and he is not buffeted by the winds of desire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਸਬਦ ਝੁਣਕਾਰੁ ਨਿਰਾਲਮੁ ਪ੍ਰਭਿ ਆਪੇ ਵਾਇ ਸੁਣਾਇਆ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: panch sabad jhunakaar niraalam prabh aape vaae sunaaeaa |8|
lines.english: God Himself plays the pure music of the Panch Shabad, the five primal sounds to hear. ||8||
Such a Yogi vibrates the unstruck sound current of the Shabad.
In the Fear of God, in detachment, one intuitively merges into the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਜਨੁ ਸਾਰਿ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਰਾਇਆ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: anjan saar niranjan jaanai sarab niranjan raaeaa |9|
lines.english: With the ointment of enlightenment, the Immaculate Lord is known; the Immaculate Lord King is pervading everywhere. ||9||
Renouncing egotism, he is imbued with the unstruck sound current.
God is eternal and imperishable; He is the Destroyer of pain and fear.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸੋ ਭਉ ਭੰਜਨੁ ਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naanak har prabh so bhau bhanjan gur miliai har prabh paaeaa |10|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord God is the Destroyer of fear; meeting the Guru, the Lord God is found. ||10||
He cures the disease, and cuts away the noose of death.
One who understands karma, realizes the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਆਪਿ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਸਭੁ ਤਿਸ ਕਾ ਚੋਜੁ ਸਬਾਇਆ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: aape jaanai aap pachhaanai sabh tis kaa choj sabaaeaa |11|
lines.english: He Himself knows, and He Himself realizes. This whole world is all His play. ||11||
One who knows the Immaculate Lord chews up death.
He Himself is the Banker, and He Himself is the Merchant.
The Appraiser Himself appraises.
God Himself, the Merciful Lord, grants His Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਕਸਿ ਕਸਵਟੀ ਲਾਏ ਆਪੇ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: aape kas kasavattee laae aape keemat paaeaa |12|
lines.english: He Himself tests upon His Touchstone, and He Himself estimates the value. ||12||
The Gardener pervades and permeates each and every heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਰਖੁ ਅਤੀਤੁ ਵਸੈ ਨਿਹਕੇਵਲੁ ਗੁਰ ਪੁਰਖੈ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਮਿਲਾਇਆ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: purakh ateet vasai nihakeval gur purakhai purakh milaaeaa |13|
lines.english: The pure, primal, detached Lord abides within all. The Guru, the Lord Incarnate, leads us to meet the Lord God. ||13||
Eradicating duality, the One Lord reveals Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਾਹਿ ਨਿਰਾਲਮੁ ਜੋਨੀ ਅਕੁਲ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਗਾਇਆ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: aasaa maeh niraalam jonee akul niranjan gaaeaa |14|
lines.english: Such a being remains unattached amidst hope, singing the Praise of the Immaculate Lord, who has no ancestry. ||14||
God is wise and all-knowing; He purges men of their pride.
He alone is spiritually wise, who contemplates his own self.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਲਾਹਾ ਸਤਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਚੁ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੫॥੨॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: naanak har jas har gun laahaa satasangat sach fal paaeaa |15|2|19|
lines.english: O Nanak, singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord, the true profit is obtained; in the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation, the fruit of Truth is obtained. ||15||2||19||
Eradicating egotism, he obtains the peace of the Shabad.
Speak the Truth, and remain in the home of Truth.
Maaroo, First Mehl:
Remain dead while yet alive, and cross over the terrifying world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਬੋਹਿਥੁ ਗੁਰੁ ਬੇੜੀ ਤੁਲਹਾ ਮਨ ਹਰਿ ਜਪਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਲੰਘਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: gur bohith gur berree tulahaa man har jap paar langhaaeaa |1|
lines.english: The Guru is the boat, the ship, the raft; meditating on the Lord in your mind, you shall be carried across to the other side. ||1||
Eliminating egotism, possessiveness and greed,
one is liberated from the nine gates, and obtains a place in the Tenth Gate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਪਰਿ ਪਰੈ ਪਰੈ ਅਪਰੰਪਰੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਆਪੇ ਆਪੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aoopar parai parai aparanpar jin aape aap upaaeaa |2|
lines.english: Lofty and high, the farthest of the far and infinite, He created Himself. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕਲੁ ਗਾਇ ਜਮ ਤੇ ਕਿਆ ਡਰੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: akal gaae jam te kiaa ddareeai |
lines.english: Singing the Praises of the absolute Lord, why should anyone be afraid of death?
Receiving the Guru's Teachings, and lovingly attuned to the Lord, one crosses over.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਚੁ ਹੈ ਸਰਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: sach har naam sach hai saranaa |
lines.english: True is the Lord's Name, and True is His Sanctuary.
True is the Word of the Guru's Shabad, grasping it, one is carries across.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਤ ਜਤ ਦੇਖਉ ਤਤ ਤਤ ਤੁਮ ਹੀ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਗਾਇਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jat jat dekhau tat tat tum hee avar na duteea gaaeaa |3|
lines.english: Wherever I look, I see only You; I do not sing of any other at all. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕਥੁ ਕਥੈ ਦੇਖੈ ਅਪਰੰਪਰੁ ਫੁਨਿ ਗਰਭਿ ਨ ਜੋਨੀ ਜਾਇਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: akath kathai dekhai aparanpar fun garabh na jonee jaaeaa |4|
lines.english: Speaking the Unspoken, one sees the Infinite Lord, and then, he does not have to enter the womb of reincarnation again. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤੁ ਸੰਤੋਖੁ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: sach bin sat santokh na paavai |
lines.english: Without the Truth, no one finds sincerity or contentment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: bin gur mukat na aavai jaavai |
lines.english: Without the Guru, no one is liberated; coming and going in reincarnation continue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਲ ਮੰਤ੍ਰੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਰਸਾਇਣੁ ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਪੂਰਾ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: mool mantru har naam rasaaein kahu naanak pooraa paaeaa |5|
lines.english: Chanting the Mool Mantra, and the Name of the Lord, the source of nectar, says Nanak, I have found the Perfect Lord. ||5||
